questions.question: A group of four people are discussing a plan to build a house. Person A suggests adding a swimming pool, person B suggests adding a garden, and person C suggests adding a tennis court. Person D finally suggests that the house should be built with a standard floor plan. What type of coordination mechanism is required to resolve this issue, and explain how it works?
questions.hint: Focus on the concept of distributed mutual exclusion

questions.question: A team of software developers are working on a project with a deadline. Two developers, Alex and Ben, have different opinions on how to implement a particular feature. Alex thinks a client-server architecture would be best, while Ben thinks a peer-to-peer architecture would be more efficient. How can coordination and agreement mechanisms be used to resolve this issue, and what are the benefits of using such mechanisms in this scenario?
questions.hint: Consider the benefits of using distributed mutual exclusion and voting mechanisms

questions.question: In a distributed system, two processes, P1 and P2, need to coordinate their actions to ensure that a shared resource is accessed safely. P1 wants to access the resource, while P2 wants to update the resource. How can a coordination mechanism be used to resolve this conflict, and what are the different types of coordination mechanisms that can be used in this scenario?
questions.hint: Focus on the different types of coordination mechanisms, such as mutual exclusion and synchronization
